name: Turkish Lamb Chops
intro: The preferred choice of protein in Iraq and across most of the Middle East is lamb. I grew up loving the smell and taste of lamb, so much so that when I realized some people don’t like how it smells and tastes, I was shocked! Lamb represents the protein of kings for us, and it’s served to guests as a way of honoring them. I find that lamb chops are probably the most accessible cut of lamb that almost everyone will love; they are known for their tenderness, succulence, and rich flavor. I would be happy to eat lamb chops simply seasoned with salt and pepper, but this Turkish-style marinade is easy to throw together and really elevates the chops. I serve them with a side of Turkish Bulgur Pilaf and a salad.

ingredients.item: lamb rib chops (about 2¼ pounds, or 1 kg) (or substitute loin chops)
ground Aleppo pepper (or substitute with ¼ teaspoon chili flakes)
sweet red pepper paste (or substitute tomato paste)

method: In a large bowl, mix the lamb chops with the lemon juice, ¼ cup (60 ml) of the oil, Aleppo pepper, cumin, coriander, oregano, salt, and red pepper paste until well combined.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let the lamb chops marinate in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour, or overnight. Let come to room temperature for at least 30 minutes before cooking them.
Heat a large cast-iron pan over medium heat. Add the remaining 2 tablespoons oil. Working in batches, add the lamb chops to the pan and cook for 2 to 3 minutes on each side for medium doneness.
Transfer the cooked chops to a plate and cover with aluminum foil. Let rest for 10 minutes before serving.
Serve the lamb chops over the bulgur pilaf with salad and yogurt.
